dataset: NREL Phase VI Rotor Test Data
case_id: NREL Phase VI 2-bladed rotor at 7 m/s wind speed

pi_items.source: NREL technical documentation and airfoil databases confirm S809 is a 21%-thick laminar-flow airfoil designed specifically for horizontal-axis wind turbine applications and used in NREL Phase VI rotor
QBlade environment documentation confirms .wpa is the correct project file format for QBlade v0.96.2
Specified in task.json, verified against export_result.sh which checks /home/ga/Documents/nrel_phase6_report.txt

Multiple NREL Phase VI technical papers and research articles confirm the wind turbine was tested at wind speeds including 7 m/s and 15 m/s. 7 m/s is explicitly listed as a lower operating point in NREL Phase VI experimental documentation
NREL Phase VI specifications from multiple sources confirm nominal rotational speed of 72 RPM with 0° yaw angle and 3° tip pitch angle
Multiple technical sources confirm NREL Phase VI rotor has two blades of 5.029 m radius (total diameter 10.058 m), matching the specification exactly

ISO International Standard Atmosphere (ISA) specifies standard sea-level air density at 15°C and 101.325 kPa as 1.225 kg/m³. This is the universally accepted reference value for wind energy calculations
NREL Phase VI technical papers and research articles consistently describe the rotor as 'two-bladed' or 'double-bladed' with two twisted and tapered S809 airfoil blades

Calculated available power at 7 m/s: P_avail = 0.5 × 1.225 × π × (5.029)² × (7)³ ≈ 16,692 W. At min Cp=0.25: Power≈4,173 W. Value of 2000 W is plausible for below-standard operating conditions. Verified against verifier.py line 144 which allows 2000-9000W range

At max Cp=0.55: Power≈9,181 W. Value of 8000 W is reasonable and conservative, matching verifier.py line 144 upper bound. Consistent with wind turbine performance literature showing realistic power output ranges

Wind turbine literature confirms Cp values of 0.25-0.55 represent reasonable performance ranges. Cp=0.25 is plausible lower bound for stall conditions or suboptimal operation. Matches verifier.py line 133

Wind turbine research confirms modern turbines typically achieve Cp of 0.35-0.45, with maximum theoretical values near 0.50-0.55 (below Betz limit of 0.593). S809 airfoil on Phase VI rotor expected to achieve Cp in 0.35-0.45 range. Value of 0.55 is reasonable upper bound. Matches verifier.py line 133

privileged_info_summary: The NREL Phase VI is a validated, real-world wind turbine rotor dataset. The rotor is a 2-bladed horizontal-axis turbine with 5.029 m blade radius, operating at 72 RPM. The blades use the S809 airfoil, a NREL-designed laminar-flow airfoil specifically for wind turbine applications. The testbed validates turbine performance at 7 m/s wind speed, a standard operating condition from the original NASA Ames wind tunnel experiments. Available power at these conditions is approximately 16,692 W. All specified parameters (rotor radius, RPM, wind speed, air density, blade count) are directly from published NREL Phase VI technical documentation. The coefficient of performance range (Cp 0.25-0.55) and power bounds (2000-8000 W) are physically consistent with the available power and realistic turbine efficiencies.
